segments.text: Sally: Excuse me, Professor.
Sally: Can I talk to you for a minute?
Tutor: Yes, do come in.
Tutor: So you're Sally Layman?
Sally: That's it.
Tutor: You want to discuss with me changing courses, right?
Sally: Yes.
Tutor: All right.
Tutor: What course are you doing at present, Sally?
Sally: It's Economic History.
Tutor: OK.
Tutor: And when did you begin this course?
Sally: Let me think.
Sally: Erm ... it was last September.
Tutor: Right.
Tutor: Good.
Tutor: Before we talk through all your problems with the course, I want to know what courses you'd like to change to.
Sally: Well, there're several courses that I've thought about, but my first choice would be Politics.
Tutor: Oh, it's one of the most popular courses in our college.
Sally: Yes, I know.
Sally: So it means there might not be places available?
Tutor: That's right.
Tutor: I'll still make a note of that.
Tutor: But it's better to give another option.
Sally: Well, I've thought of changing the course entirely to Business Studies, but then I realize it is new to me, so I think Philosophy might be a better choice.
Tutor: OK, that's useful to know.
Tutor: Then let's talk through any problems you've been having at the moment and see if there is any possibility for us to offer help.
Tutor: After all, with all the efforts that you've made already, it'll be a pity to give up the present course and completely start a new one.
Sally: Yes, I agree with that.
Sally: I've learnt something valuable about Economic History.
Tutor: Good.
Tutor: Well, let's talk about your problems.
Tutor: What do you think is your significant difficulty in the course?
Sally: Well, I have to say the biggest problem is that the lectures are difficult.
Tutor: OK.
Tutor: And have you tried something to overcome this problem?
Sally: Yes, I have.
Sally: I tried recording the lectures because I couldn't follow what the lecturer said.
Tutor: Yes, that's a good idea.
Tutor: And did you find it helped?
Sally: To a certain extent, but ...
Tutor: Well, recording is a good strategy, but I also advise you to take part in the discussion group with other fellow students to discuss the lectures and exchange ideas with them.
Sally: Yes, I've seen some of my classmates doing that.
Sally: I'll ask them if I can join.
Tutor: Good, I'm sure you'll find it helps.
Tutor: And any other problems?
Sally: Yes, the second problem is, I think there aren't enough tutorials for this course.
Tutor: OK.
Tutor: I think you can talk to your tutor about this.
Sally: Yes, I did that.
Sally: But the tutor is so busy that I must wait until she is available.
Tutor: Why don't you try arranging meetings ahead of time?
Sally: Well, I can try this way.
Tutor: Good.
Sally: But I suppose the thing that made me upset recently is my poor assignment grade.
Tutor: Oh, the grade is an important issue.
Sally: Well, I think I've tried my best to achieve a good score.
Sally: I did more background reading than before for the last assignment we just submitted.
Tutor: Good, and did you feel that helped?
Sally: Oh yes, I think so.
Tutor: Well, I think you can try another strategy, which is to visit student services, which offer a lot of helpful advice on assignment preparation.
Sally: Oh, I've never heard about that.
Tutor: I'll give you a booklet with details on this.
Tutor: You'll find it well worth trying.
Sally: OK, I'll certainly try it.
Tutor: OK, that's all the problem.
Tutor: Well, I've made a record of our discussions and suggested strategies.
Tutor: Typically, we advise you to try out these suggestions for a short period and then come back to discuss the situation to see whether it is improved.
Tutor: If not, we will start considering alternative courses.
Sally: OK, when shall I come back?
Tutor: How about in a-month time?
Sally: OK.
Tutor: That will be on the 17th of February.
Tutor: How about half past three in the afternoon?
Tutor: Is that all right for you?
Sally: Yes, sure.
Sally: And where shall I meet you?
Tutor: I'm afraid I will be on leave at that time.
Tutor: But I'll leave this record to our senior adviser, and you will meet with him then in this office.
Sally: OK.
Tutor: Good, that's it!
Tutor: Let's see whether things can improve, Sally.
Sally: OK.
Sally: Thanks very much!
Tutor: My pleasure.
Tutor: Bye!
